mechanism_narrative: A sustained 3-month decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 1-month rise in the unemployment rate above 0.3 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions, which weakens corporate earnings expectations and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in >60% of triggered instances despite negative signal.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent declines in GDP and industrial production indicate a contraction in economic activity, which reduces corporate profitability and generates negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5d returns in >60% of instances where both GDPC1 and INDPRO decline.

mechanism_narrative: In high inflation regimes (CPIAUCSL > 2.5%), rising federal funds rates signal tightening monetary policy that increases equity discount rates, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: In high inflation periods, FEDFUNDS increases fail to produce negative returns (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A sustained 3-month increase in housing starts signals stronger consumer demand and corporate investment, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in >60% of instances where HOUST increases.

mechanism_narrative: A 1-month decline in consumer credit (RSAFS) above 1% signals reduced consumer spending, which weakens corporate revenue growth and generates negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in >60% of triggered instances despite negative signal.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in payroll employment and unemployment rate indicate labor market mismatch, which raises uncertainty about corporate earnings and generates negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60d returns in >60% of instances where both PAYEMS and UNRATE rise.

mechanism_narrative: In supply contraction regimes (INDPRO falls >2% in 3 months), rising federal funds rates signal tighter monetary policy that exacerbates corporate cost pressures, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: In supply contraction periods, FEDFUNDS increases fail to produce negative returns (t-test p > 0.05).
